title: minuartia verna subsp. kabylica
intro: Minuartia verna subsp. kabylica, also known as the North African spring-crowfoot, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Minuartia verna subsp. kabylica is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to leverage its medicinal properties.
